title: Cancellation on SSE client disconnect (compute + writer)
description: When the SSE client disconnects (browser tab closed, Cancel button), both stages stop cleanly. Compute drains in-flight futures; writer flushes current track then exits. No orphaned threads, no half-committed transactions.
acceptanceCriteria: _check_disconnect background task polls request.is_disconnected() every 200ms; on disconnect sets cancel_event.
_compute_stage stops submitting new futures on cancel; lets in-flight futures resolve.
_writer_stage processes whatever remains in queue then exits on sentinel.
Final 'cancelled' SSE event emitted before stream closes (if client still listening).
tests/test_serve_routes.py: simulate client disconnect mid-stream; assert thread exits within 500ms; assert no exceptions in server logs.

steps.details: async def _poll_disconnect(request, cancel_event): while not cancel_event.is_set(): if await request.is_disconnected(): cancel_event.set(); return; await asyncio.sleep(0.2). Spawn as asyncio.create_task in the SSE generator.

After each as_completed result, check cancel_event.is_set(); if set, stop submitting more futures (early-exit the submit loop). Already-submitted futures keep running until they finish (don't future.cancel() - lets them complete).
After each queue.get() + commit, check cancel_event.is_set(); if set, drain remaining queue items (commit each) then exit. The point is: data already computed should still be saved.
When the writer exits, emit {done: true, cancelled: cancel_event.is_set(), processed: N, errors: M}. May not reach the client if connection already closed - that's fine.
tests/test_serve_routes.py: open SSE; read 5 events; close httpx client; sleep 600ms; assert writer thread.is_alive() == False; assert any in-flight cues were committed (not orphaned).

technicalNotes: The 'finish what you started' semantics matter: a partial cancellation that loses 8 minutes of compute work would be a regression vs the serial v0 path which had no cancel at all.
request.is_disconnected() is async; the cancel_event is threading - bridge them via the poll task.
